text.title: Account Health Scoring Model (Sub-20 Accounts) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Account Health Scoring Model (Sub-20 Accounts) plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: Health score MUST aggregate exactly six observable signals (utilization variance, decision-maker engagement, scope-creep delta, payment punctuality, referral/advocacy, sponsor stability) with each signal scored 0/1/2.
rule.rationale: NPS-only or MRR-only scoring fails at micro-agency scale where each client is statistically unique; six signals balance leading + lagging indicators.

rule.statement: Every signal MUST be sourced from a system of record (timesheet, billing, calendar, repo, CRM); founder gut-feel signals are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Founder optimism bias inflates scores on the relationships that need attention most; observable signals are the only auditable input.

rule.statement: Tier mapping MUST use fixed cutoffs (green 10-12, yellow 5-9, red 0-4); per-account custom thresholds are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Per-account thresholds cause score laundering — the founder slides the cutoff to keep favourite accounts green; fixed cutoffs are non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Scoring MUST run weekly on Mondays; biweekly cadence is allowed only if all six signal sources update less than weekly.
rule.rationale: Monthly cadence misses 4 weeks of leading-indicator drift; weekly is the minimum cadence that lets retention actions land before churn.

rule.statement: Retention action MUST trigger when score drops 2+ in a week OR sits red for 2 consecutive weeks; triggers without action are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Scoring without action is theatre; the trigger condition makes the score operationally useful rather than a dashboard ornament.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
